sector_definitions.Agriculture fishing and forestry: Farming, livestock, fisheries, forestry, irrigation — the production of food and raw materials from land and water. Classify here when the paper studies agricultural production, crop yields, livestock, forestry management, or irrigation as the primary subject.
sector_definitions.Education: Schooling, teaching, learning outcomes, educational institutions, curriculum, school management — the delivery and quality of education itself. Classify here when the paper studies what happens inside schools or education systems as the primary outcome.
sector_definitions.Energy and extractives: Power generation, electricity, oil, gas, mining, renewable energy, energy efficiency, extractive industries. Classify here when the paper studies energy production, extraction of resources, or energy infrastructure as the primary subject — NOT when energy is only a keyword or setting for a policy/governance study.
sector_definitions.Financial sector: Banking, credit, monetary policy, financial markets, insurance, financial regulation. Classify here when the paper studies the financial system itself — monetary policy, banking access, financial markets — NOT when a financial mechanism (like cash transfers or microfinance) is used as a welfare intervention (that is Social protection).
sector_definitions.Health: Medical care, public health, disease, nutrition, healthcare delivery, health outcomes. Classify here when the paper studies health outcomes or healthcare systems as the primary subject — NOT when health is merely the outcome of a welfare/safety-net program (that is Social protection).
sector_definitions.Social protection: Welfare, safety nets, cash transfers, social insurance, labor market programs, pensions, food-for-work, conditional cash transfers. Classify here when the paper studies a program that provides financial or in-kind support to vulnerable populations — EVEN IF the measured outcome is health, nutrition, education, or employment. The key question: is the paper about a welfare/safety-net intervention? If yes, this is Social protection, not the outcome domain.
sector_definitions.Industry trade and services: Manufacturing, trade, commerce, firm productivity, industrial policy, business development, services sector. Classify here when the paper studies firms, trade, manufacturing, or industry as the primary subject — NOT when a jobs program places workers with firms (that is Social protection).
sector_definitions.Information and communications technolog: Telecommunications, internet, digital infrastructure, ICT for development, mobile technology. Classify here when the paper studies digital or communications infrastructure as the primary subject.
sector_definitions.Public administration: Governance, institutions, public sector management, property rights, legal systems, anti-corruption, decentralization, transparency, tax administration. Classify here when the paper studies government institutions, legal frameworks, or administrative systems as the primary subject — NOT when a government program delivers welfare (that is Social protection) or agriculture (that is Agriculture).
sector_definitions.Transportation: Roads, transport infrastructure, logistics, public transit, ports, railways. Classify here when the paper studies physical transportation infrastructure or logistics systems as the primary subject.
sector_definitions.Water sanitation and waste management: Water supply, sanitation, wastewater, solid waste, water quality, water treatment. Classify here when the paper studies water/sanitation infrastructure or environmental contamination as the primary subject — NOT when health effects of contaminated water are merely the motivation (that is still Water/sanitation).

Agriculture fishing and forestry: Agricultural extension, research and other support activities
Public admin - Agriculture, fishing and forestry
Industry trade and services: Public admin - Industry, trade and services
Information and communications technolog: Public admin - Information and communications technologies
Water sanitation and waste management: Public admin - Water, sanitation and waste management
Other - Water, sanitation and waste management
